title: thymus serpyllum var. ocheus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. ocheus, commonly known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading herbaceous perennial plant native to Europe, with tiny pink, white, or purple flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs, colds, and bronchitis. The health benefits of creeping thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and act as a natural expectorant. Creeping thyme can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in aromatherapy, where its essential oil is inhaled to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
